title: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK
summary: Expanding our Global Esports Distribution with KICK  Riot Games
content:
We’re welcoming KICK to our global roster of streaming platforms, giving players around the world more ways to watch, co-stream, and engage with our games and esports. KICK is the primary platform for many passionate esports fans, especially in LATAM, MENA, and Europe. Bringing KICK into the mix of our global platform lineup helps us better serve those communities and brings more players together around the moments, matches, and creators they care about most.

Starting with MSI 2026, KICK will carry live broadcasts for global and regional events across LoL Esports, VALORANT, and TFT (excluding China and Korea).

In addition to expanding where fans can watch, this partnership also opens up more opportunities for creators. KICK streamers will have new ways to grow their communities and connect with Riot through ourexpanding creator and partner programsacross League, VALORANT, TFT, and Wild Rift.

The viewing experience on KICK will feel familiar to players, with access to the same drops and incentives available during our global event broadcasts on other platforms. As for KICK  streamers, we’ll also apply the same moderation practices and standards creators on other platforms are held to, ensuring a safe and positive experience for all.

title: C9, FLY, and LOS invited to pres-MSI cross-regional showdown against LCK teams, hosted by SOOP
summary: C9, FLY, and LOS invited to pres-MSI cross-regional showdown against LCK teams, hosted by SOOP  Sheep Esports
content:
Your premier source for esports news, exclusive leaks, and expert analysis. Stay ahead of the game with the latest updates from League of Legends, Valorant, CS2, and Rocket League.

With just a few hours to spare before the Mid-Season Invitational, SOOP is debuting its own event, featuring American teams facing off against lower-tier Korean teams.

The Sheep Esports team is composed of multiple writers and reporters

title: Everything You Need To Know About LCP 2026 Wild Card
summary: Everything You Need To Know About LCP 2026 Wild Card  League of Legends
content:
The LCP Wild Card makes its return this year as teams across Southeast Asia once again compete for qualification into the LCP Promotion Series, for the opportunity to be promoted into the LCP!

In line with our goal of providing wider access to the LCP, the Wild Card this year will host qualifiers in a number of regions including Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Philippines, and Thailand.

In addition to the regional qualifiers, the final stage of qualification will be the Last Chance Qualifier, open to eligible teams from across Southeast Asia. Teams that participated in previous Wild Card Qualifiers, as well as teams from other eligible regions, may also enter the Last Chance Qualifier.

The winner of each regional qualifier, including the Last Chance Qualifier, will advance to the LCP Wild Card Playoffs, for a total of four qualified teams. The winner of the Playoffs will then move on to the LCP Promotion Series and compete for a spot in the LCP next year!

PARTICIPATING REGIONSRegions and their corresponding tournaments that are enrolled into the LCP Wild Card:

title: Riot Games Adds KICK To Its Streaming Platform Network For MSI 2026
summary: Riot Games Adds KICK To Its Streaming Platform Network For MSI 2026  Hotspawn
content:
With only two days away from the start of the Mid-Season Invitational, Riot Games has announced the addition of streaming platform KICK to its global distribution network.

According to a release, the game publisher allows KICK to carry live broadcasts for both global and regional events acrossLoL Esports, VALORANT, and TFT, starting withMSI.

The focus will be primarily on the LATAM, MENA, and European regions, with both China and Korea. The partnership will also allow KICK streamers to grow their community and connect with Riot through the expanding creator and partner programs across several game titles, including Wild Rift.

Users will also gain access to drops and incentives during live event broadcasts, just like it works on other streaming platforms.

Founded at the end of 2022, KICK is one of the newer streaming platforms in the space and is considered a direct alternative to Twitch.

Backed by the owners of Stake, an online gambling site co-founded by Ed Craven and Bijan Tehrani, KICK made headlines for the high shares of subscription revenues given to creators, as well as the possibility to stream gambling content.

title: These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters
summary: These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters  Red Bull
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

In Europe's LEC, the atmosphere is electric as teams prepare to integrate these updates into their drafts. G2 Esports and Fnatic have historically led the charge in innovative pocket picks and creative map rotations. This new development could provide the perfect opportunity for rising rosters to challenge the established order and secure crucial championship points for the upcoming international events.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'These iconic moments prove why LoL's Mid-Season Invitational matters  Red Bull' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.
